Furthermore, we will promote personnel exchange and mobility among industry, academia, and government, both domestically and internationally, including talent matching between industry and academia (e.g., internship programs) and cross-disciplinary personnel exchanges.
- ✓ Furthermore, national R&D projects will actively establish mechanisms and set themes that enable participation from personnel across diverse fields.
- ✓ Furthermore, we will enhance and strengthen outreach activities, such as educational programs and science museum exhibitions, targeting younger generations in junior high and high schools, who are the source of industrial talent.

Furthermore, by linking these with quantum computers and conventional (classical) computers both domestically and internationally, we will build and provide cutting-edge quantum-classical hybrid computing environments that lead industrialization through practical application environments and pioneer new frontiers in industry and science.

Support for Use Case Development, etc.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Examination of Performance and Effectiveness Metrics for Quantum Technology Utilization from the User Company Perspective

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ To promote the adoption and investment in quantum computers across diverse industries, it is crucial to demonstrate to user company executives and investors how quantum computer utilization can impact business activities. This demonstration should include performance comparisons with existing technologies, as well as perspectives from management viewpoints (e.g., ROI) and ESG viewpoints (including SDGs and decarbonization).
- ✓ Therefore, to enable user companies to understand and judge whether quantum computers offer performance advantages over existing technologies, we will examine performance metrics for quantum computers, software (algorithms), and quantum-classical hybrid systems (advantages over existing technologies, benchmarks in real-world use, etc.), incorporating results from use cases.
✓ Furthermore, from a management and ESG perspective, we will evaluate the effects of quantum computer utilization on business activities, encompassing not only performance but also cost, convenience, and decarbonization.
Effectiveness metrics capable of appropriately evaluating outcomes (including future effects) will also be examined. Furthermore, mechanisms will be developed to enable executives, investors, and others to accurately assess business activities utilizing quantum computers and similar technologies, thereby facilitating informed management and investment decisions.
- ✓ Furthermore, we will advance the development of mechanisms (such as performance comparison software) enabling user companies to compare the performance of multiple quantum computers and software. When considering performance metrics, we will also factor in the potential for future technological advancements to improve these metrics, given the current limitations of quantum computer performance.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### ② Promotion of the Software Industry

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇 Cultivating and Promoting Software and Application Support Service Providers

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Quantum computers often require different middleware connecting applications to the quantum computer, as well as different software development environments (such as Software Development Kits (SDKs)), depending on the specific machine or platform. Furthermore, a certain level of specialized knowledge is often required, making the barrier to entry high for general users.
- ✓ To expand quantum computer usage in the future, it is crucial to create an environment where users can seamlessly switch between diverse machines without conscious awareness. Furthermore, from a future practical application perspective, an integrated computing environment (quantum-classical hybrid computing environment) that intelligently selects and utilizes quantum computers or conventional (classical) computers in the background for specific computational tasks is also anticipated.
- ✓ Therefore, we will build and provide an environment where users can easily utilize quantum-classical hybrid computing environments. This includes developing common application software, middleware, SDKs, and other development tools that can handle multiple quantum computers in combination with classical computers.
- ✓ Furthermore, considering that the processing on the classical computer side used to control quantum computers is rapidly becoming more sophisticated as quantum computer performance improves, the integrated use of quantum computers and high-performance computing (HPC).
✓ In recentyears,private companies have become increasingly active in providing comprehensive usage support services, including consulting and education, alongside software services that offer applications, middleware, development environments, and other tools to assist users. In Japan, many of these private companies are venture firms. They are key players contributing to improving quantum computer usability and serving as hubs connecting users with quantum computing utilization.
When the government advances the development of quantum computer usage environments as described above, it will maximize the utilization of these private companies to establish and provide highly convenient usage environments for users, while fostering and promoting software and usage support service providers.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### ③ Establishing a Quantum Computer Utilization Environment for Software Development 〇Establishing Flagship Computing Environments to Pioneer New Frontiers

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ InMarch2023,theNationalInstituteforBasicBiology(hereinafterreferredtoas"RIKEN") announced and began operating Japan's first domestically developed prototype machine (a superconducting quantum computer testbed).
- ✓ Leveragingthe characteristics of this domestically developed hardware,this testbed enablescontroldown to deep layers of the hardware. Following its operational launch, it will foster and advance core technologies spanning software (error suppression/correction, etc.), middleware (architecture/systems), and hardware (control devices, etc.), as well as develop quantum-classical hybrid technologies. This will create an environment usable by industry, academia, and government for diverse applications.
- ✓ Furthermore, RIKEN will advance the development of an Advanced Computation Hub. This hub will provide cutting-edge quantum-classical hybrid computing environments, including this testbed, integrated with state-of-the-art quantum computers and conventional (classical) computing systems like Fugaku. It will involve not only the quantum technology community but also computational science and mathematical science communities, pioneering new frontiers in industry and science.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Establishing Practical Computational Environments to Lead Industrialization

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ To foster the creation of quantum computer use cases and promote industrial adoption, we will establish a practical quantum-classical hybrid computing environment that leads industrialization and is easily accessible to a broad range of users.
- ✓ In the near term, we will utilize simulated annealing machines, followed by quantum computers (annealing-based). Furthermore, we will consider industrial applications for quantum computers (gate-based) from a medium-to-long-term perspective.
- ✓ Furthermore, the utilization of diverse types of commercially available quantum computers 5(<>)from overseas manufacturers, which are leading in commercialization, is also beneficial. We will carefully examine and select the actual machines necessary for creating use cases based on userneeds and effectiveness, and utilize them flexibly.

Japanese industry can leverage its strengths, as well as critical technology areas based on this assessment. We will work to secure and advance the necessary equipment, components, materials, andtechnologiesinthesefields.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Supporting Commercialization of Private Machines and Enhancing International

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### Competitiveness

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Although superconducting quantum computers have been released commercially, the superconducting approach, like other approaches, is not yet a mature technology. It requires long-term research and development, and achieving practical application and large-scale deployment demands enormous costs.Consequently, while some point out that relying solely on private companies for R&D, practical application, and industrialization carries high risk, this also presents a business opportunity for quantum computer vendors and suppliers of related components and materials to create new markets.
- ✓ Therefore, the National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology (AIST) will advance the creation of an environment that supports private sector activities in fundamental research on elemental technologies such as quantum chips, and in processes for prototyping, testing, and evaluation aimed at scaling up (e.g., prototyping, testing, and evaluation of quantum chips; testing peripheral components under cryogenic conditions).
- ✓ Furthermore, at the main hubs—RIKEN and AIST—RIKEN will support research and development at the fundamental stage, while AIST will support industry in the prototyping, testing, and evaluation processes for scaling up. By clarifying such roles and working together, both institutions will cooperate and collaborate to support private sector activities.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### <Other Gate Technologies (Ion Trap, Optical, Silicon, Atomic, etc.)>

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### Promoting Industry Participation and Strengthening Collaborative Systems

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ InJapan, alongside superconductingmethods,research and development for other approaches such as ion traps, optical, silicon, and atomic methods is being strongly accelerated and promoted, primarily through national projects (e.g., the Moonshot R&D Program).
- ✓ Overseas, private companies are leading commercialization efforts across various technical approaches. Japan will actively build partnerships with private companies, including startups, to promote their participation and activities, aiming for future practical application and industrialization.
✓ Furthermore, Japan's first startup in the quantum computer (hardware) field was established in 2022 and plans to develop and release quantum computers/quantum networks going forward. For startups/ventures advancing cutting-edge technologies requiring long-term investment and carrying high risk, utilizing national project support is also an effective means. Therefore, to bolster the activities of these startups/ventures, we will enhance support mechanisms and build frameworks through national projects.
Therefore, to support the activities of such startups and venture companies, we will enhance support throughnational projects and establish mechanisms.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### ② Quantum Computers (Annealing Method)

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Support for Technology Development and Commercialization of Quantum Annealing Machines

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Developing quantum annealing machines—which are nearing practical application and industrialization in Japan—and creating new markets and expanding businesses utilizing them holds significant importance. This approach enables the rapid formation of a market for the quantum computing industry and realizes a sustainable funding cycle (ecosystem) where profits from industrial applications are reinvested into technological R&D.
- ✓ Domestic companies are collaborating with AIST to develop quantum annealing machines, with plans to unveil a prototype around fiscal year 2024 and aim for a 1,000-qubit-class machine by fiscal year 2027.
- ✓ Currently, Canada's D-Wave Systems leads with commercial services for machines around 5,000 qubits. However, qubit scale does not necessarily determine performance. Therefore, we will clarify quantitative performance advantages, advance support for early prototype verification, and steadily progress technical development and commercialization support.
- ✓ Furthermore,by integrating with conventional(classical)computers to tackle large-scale problems, and by operating in unison with diverse computing resources such as HPC and simulated annealing machines, we will utilize this as a practical computing environment leading industrial applications. This will enhance competitive advantage in industrial utilization.

text: ### ③ Common Foundational Technologies

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇 Securing and Advancing Essential Components for Global Quantum Computer Commercialization and Strengthening the Supply Chain

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
6)
- ✓ To steadily advance the R&D and commercialization of quantum computers, establishing a strategic supply chain that stably supplies high-performance devices, components, and materials is indispensable. For quantum computers, roadmaps toward scaling up have been presented by various companies for the 2020s (. Realizing these systems requires building stable and resilient supply chains for related equipment, components, materials, and more.
✓ Therefore, anticipating future large-scale quantum computer systems, the necessary equipment, components, and materials must be secured.
We will identify the key industrial sectors and companies, including SMEs, that will play a leading role in this endeavor, and promote their participation.Concurrently, we will identify priority technology fields where Japanese industry can leverage its strengths, as well as strategically importantchoke point technologies within the supplychain. We will then develop and implement strategies to secure and advance the necessary equipment, components, materials, and technologies in these fields. This will encourage participation from numerous companies in existing electronics sectors and other fields. Through collaboration between domestic and like-minded international companies, we will build a stable and resilient global supply chain.
- ✓ The practical application and scaling up of quantum computers require the development and commercialization of diverse components and materials. However, the enormous costs and human resources involved create barriers to entry for new companies.Therefore, AIST will promote the establishment of a global supply chain by supporting the activities of private companies, such as component and material suppliers and quantum computer vendors. This support will be provided through R&Doncoretechnologieslikequantumchips, prototyping of controldevices, peripheral components, and materials forlarge-scaleimplementation,andevaluation undercryogenic conditions.

This includes promoting the use of quantum security in rules and guidelines for each field, based on these use cases. We will also support public institutions, such as local governments, in promoting adoption as anchor tenants/early adopters.

Furthermore, another domestic vendor is scheduled to enter the market and release a commercial device in fiscal year 2023 or later.
✓ To bolster the efforts of domestic vendors, we will actively support technological development through operations in the aforementioned wide-area testbeds. Furthermore, we will steadily accumulate operational and usage (use case) track records and leverage these
utilizing this accumulated experience, the public and private sectors will unite to support overseas expansion. Furthermore, we will vigorously advance standardization and intellectual property development to promote deployment and adoption both domestically and internationally.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Promoting the Establishment of a Domestic Certification Infrastructure for Quantum

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### Cryptography Communication Equipment

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ In recent years, serious domestic cases where cyberattacks have made business continuity difficult have been increasingly observed, making security assurance an extremely critical issue for users. Given the importance of ensuring security, a mechanism is required that allows users to determine whether quantum cryptography devices reliably guarantee the performance they demand when introducing them.Furthermore, starting in fiscal year 2023, multiple domestic vendors will offer quantum cryptography communication devices. Users also require a mechanism to objectively compare the performance of these devices using standardized metrics.
- ✓ Considering these points, to expand the use and adoption of quantum cryptography communication devices, establishing a third-party device certification system is necessary. Therefore, alongside examining the certification system, efforts will be made to build a domestic certification infrastructure, including the operational and management framework for evaluation bodies.
- ✓ In doing so, we will establish mechanisms to utilize private companies as evaluation bodies, fostering an autonomous ecosystem for the certification system, and cultivate and secure the necessary personnel. Furthermore, when considering the certification system, we will pay attention to international cooperation and coordination at the institutional level, with an eye toward future overseas expansion.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### ② Enhancing the Utilization Environment for Quantum Security Networks and Expanding Practical

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:

- ✓ In doing so, it is crucial to adopt a perspective that complements the strengths and weaknesses of diverse quantum and classical cryptographic methods—such as quantum cryptography, quantum streaming cryptography, post-quantum cryptography (PQC), secret sharing, and secure multi-party computation—to explore the optimal mix. ( P Q C ), secret sharing, and secure computation.
- ✓ To establish an environment capable of verifying this optimal mix of quantum and classical technologies, we will construct and validate a quantum integrated architecture (quantum technology platform) encompassing quantum cryptography, quantum-secure cloud, quantum computers, and other elements.

- ✓ To further expand various use cases, we will construct an inter-city quantum cryptography communication network that includes space assets such as satellites, and subsequently scale it from inter-city to nationwide coverage.
- ✓ In doing so, wewillalsofocusonbuildingandadvancingthetestbedtocontributeto creating use cases that achieve the optimal mix of quantum and classical technologies.

text: ### 〇 Research and Development on Quantum Internet

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ The quantum internet, which enables long-distance communication using quantum states, is anticipated not only for highly secure quantum cryptography but also as a future technology enabling large-scale computations byconnecting multiple quantumcomputers.
- ✓ Furthermore,newstartups are emerging torealizethefuturequantuminternet.
- ✓ With an eye toward fostering future industries, including these startups, we will advance R&D and technical demonstrations covering devices, architectures, and protocols. We will also proceed with studies to concretize a roadmap for introducing quantum internet.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ## (4) Quantum Measurement/Sensing and Quantum Materials

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ To promote the quantum measurement and sensing industry, we will actively provide information on quantum measurement and sensing technologies and their applications to a wide range of industries, support technology development and commercialization, and establish frameworks such as industry-academia-government consortia where stakeholderscan shareinformationandexchangeopinions.
- ✓ We will prepare and provide an environment enabling many companies to easily utilize and develop quantum measurement and sensing, along with technical and application support. Furthermore, we will actively support the creation of compelling, appealing use cases for users and the development and demonstration of technologies for commercialization.
- ✓ Under the participation of the materials industry, promote a framework enabling integrated hardware and software development for technological advancement and commercialization. Furthermore, establish a system where industry, academia, and government collaborate to ensure a stable supply of quantum materials strategically vital for Japan.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### ① Promotion of the Quantum Measurement and Sensing Industry

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇 Support for Technology Development and Commercialization in Quantum Measurement and Sensing

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Quantum measurement and sensing has diverse applications across fields such as healthcare, energy, communications, mobility, and security. It is crucial to advance technology development and commercialization with the participation of numerous companies.
- ✓ Therefore, key hubs such as the Quantum Technology Infrastructure Hub (tentative name), the Quantum Life Hub (National Institute for Quantum Science and Technology (QST)),and the Quantum Sensor Hub (Tokyo Institute of Technology) will take the lead. They will actively provide information to a broad range of industry stakeholders, including users and vendors, on quantum measurement and sensing technologies and their applications, including their stage of practical implementation and advantages over existing technologies. They will also support technology development and commercialization.Furthermore, beyond pursuing top-tier performance in fundamental research, industry, academia, and government will collaborate to advance technology development and commercialization, including engineering technologies for practical implementation such as miniaturization, modularization, and chip integration. Progress will also be made with an integrated perspective, considering the fusion of quantum measurement and sensing into existing systems.
- ✓ To encourage participation from numerous companies and realize an industrial ecosystem where multiple companies collaborate and cooperate (horizontal division of labor, vertical integration, etc.), we will also promote the establishment of structures such as industry-academia-government consortia where diverse stakeholders can share information and exchange opinions.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### Providing Utilization and Development Environments for Quantum Measurement and Sensing

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
✓ For quantum measurement and sensing technologies, the technical barriers are high for many companies.
Furthermore, unlike quantum computers, many require hardware, making technical and application support crucial.
- ✓ Therefore, major hubs such as the Quantum Technology Infrastructure Hub (tentative name)and the Quantum Life Hub (Quantum Research Organization) will take the lead in establishing and providing an open environment where numerous companies, including users and vendors, can easily utilize and develop quantum measurement and sensing technologies (particularly solid-state quantum sensors, hyperpolarization technology, optical lattice clock networks, etc., applicable to a wide range of industrial uses). They will also advance necessary technical and application support based on user needs.
✓ Furthermore, to enable the full-scale utilization and dissemination of quantum measurement and sensing, efforts will be promoted to standardize quantum measurement and sensing technologies and to establish measurement standards utilizing quantum measurement and sensing.
○ Expanding and Promoting User Industries
- ✓ To promote the quantum measurement and sensing industry, it is crucial to expand the user market by discovering and increasing the number of various user companies utilizing quantum measurement and sensing.
- ✓ Therefore, we will actively support the development and demonstration of technologies for creating compelling use cases and commercialization that appeal to many users, including through the provision of quantum measurement and sensing utilization environments.
- ✓ In doing so, we will proactively provide accurate and easy-to-understand information, including the performance of quantum measurement and sensing, its advantages and effectiveness over existing sensing technologies (performance, cost, convenience, etc.).

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### ② Promotion of the Quantum Materials Industry

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Support for Technology Development and Commercialization of Quantum Materials

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Quantum materials are foundational technologies for quantum computers, quantum measurement and sensing, and quantum networks. Furthermore, the materials field, including quantum materials, is an area where Japanese industry can leverage its strengths.However, it is also pointed out that the barriers to entry into the quantum materials industry are high at present. This is because quantum technology is not yet established, and use cases and markets are not yet defined, making it difficult to forecast sales and assess business risks.
- ✓ Therefore, when conducting R&D on quantum technologies and developing use cases, we will strive to identify and demonstrate use cases with early realization potential. Simultaneously, we will clarify future market prospects and impacts from an early stage and disseminate this information widely to relevant industries.
- ✓ Furthermore, to expedite industrialization efforts, we will promote the participation of private companies that could become key players in the quantum materials field from the earliest stages. This will establish an integrated framework where hardware, software, and materials development are advanced together.
and software to drive technological development.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Building a Quantum Materials Supply Chain

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Quantum materials form the foundation of quantum technology. It is crucial for Japan to establish stable and strategic supply chains for key quantum materials that must be secured.
- ✓ To this end, the Quantum and AI Convergence Technology Business Development Global Hub (AIST),the Quantum Technology Infrastructure Center (tentative name)(Japan Atomic Energy Agency), and the Quantum Materials Center (National Institute for Materials Science), among others. Industry, academia, and government will collaborate to identify quantum materials Japan should strategically secure based on user needs, then establish a system for their stable supply.
- ✓ Furthermore, with an eye toward the future quantum industry, efforts will be made to form an industrial ecosystem where quantum materials (upstream) and quantum device-related industries (downstream) collaborate and cooperate.

text: ## (5) Innovation Infrastructure

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Global Collaboration and Expansion of the Quantum Industry

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Given the anticipated massive global market for quantum industries in the future, securing overseas markets requires leveraging Memorandums of Understanding (MoUs) 8(<>)between quantum industry organizations and the global networks 9(<>)of private companies. It is crucial to promote global collaboration (technical cooperation, business partnerships, etc.)and overseas expansion across industries through integrated public-private efforts.Furthermore, with an eye toward future overseas expansion, actively demonstrating services overseas (in Europe, the Americas, Asia, etc.) is also effective.
- ✓ Therefore, leveraging the know-how and track record accumulated through the utilization and technical demonstration of quantum technologies, including the leading QKD network, the public and private sectors will work together to promote the international expansion of products and services and global information dissemination. In doing so, local networks will also be utilized through foreign government agencies (such as embassies in Japan).Furthermore, since small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), including startups and venture companies, often lack opportunities for overseas expansion, we will provide detailed support such asassistance withexhibiting atoverseas trade showsanddisseminating information.
- ✓ Furthermore, through international cooperation, dialogue, and exchange across various levels of industry, academia, and government, we will work toward global industrial collaboration, overseas market expansion, and the acquisition of international standards.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ## 〇Establishing Foundations for Creating Startups/Venture Companies and New Businesses

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ In Japan, both software and hardware startups/venture companies (including new business divisions of existing companies and carve-out ventures) are increasing.
- ✓ As the quantum technology market requires long-term investment, it is crucial for industry, academia, and government to work together to create and support startups and venture companies, enhancing various forms of support tailored to each stage and characteristic, with the goal of forming a future venture ecosystem.
✓ Therefore, it is essential to foster and support startups through matching with financial institutions, incubators, partner companies, etc.; cultivate young entrepreneurial talent to lead these startups; facilitate talent matching (e.g., between research personnel and management talent); and develop businesses utilizing quantum technology.
We will establish a comprehensive innovation foundation to create and support startups through close collaboration among industry, academia, and government. This includes mechanisms for discovering and creating ideas (pitch contests, ideathons/hackathons, etc.), forming ecosystems through partnerships with existing companies and collaboration among startups, disseminating information about business activities domestically and internationally, and supporting overseas expansion.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇 Cultivating and Securing Industrial Talent

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ The global shortage of talent in quantum technology fields is intensifying competition for personnel. It is crucial for industry, academia, and government to unite, collaborating with like-minded nations, to strategically cultivate and secure industrial talent in quantum technology fields over the long term.
- ✓ Looking ahead to future industries, it is vital to cultivate and secure not only research and technical personnel in quantum technology but also talent in related fields (devices/components/materials, electronics,semiconductors, architecture/systems, software, information and communications, etc.). Furthermore, it is necessary to cultivate and secure personnel capable of effectively utilizing quantum technology in user-sector industries through reskilling and similar measures.Furthermore, for the commercialization and industrialization of quantum technology, it is also necessary to cultivate and secure business personnel, such as those with expertise in management, intellectual property, and law. Additionally, for DX-related industries in fields like AI, cloud computing, and IoT, synergistic effects are expected through collaboration with quantum computers and similar technologies. Therefore, reskilling personnel in these industries is also considered effective.
- ✓ Therefore, we will vigorously promote educational support for developing and securing industrial talent. This involves defining the necessary skills for personnel across these industrial sectors, developing effective educational programs based on these definitions, and providing these programs to personnel at various levels within the industries.Furthermore, we will enhance outreach activities, such as educational programs and science museum exhibits, targeting younger generations—including university students and junior/senior high school students who could become future industrial talent—to provide seamless educational opportunities from youth to industry.
- ✓ We will also explore mechanisms to expand employment and entrepreneurship opportunities by guaranteeing the quality of human resources through certification systems for those who complete educational programs.
- ✓ Furthermore, we will foster a talent development ecosystem by promoting talent matching between industry and academia and across different fields (e.g., industry-academia talent matching events, talent pools and matching mechanisms for executives and engineers, internship programs for students tackling industrial challenges), as well as talent exchange and mobility among industry, academia, and government, both domestically and internationally.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ## 〇 Building a New Partnership Framework for Industry-Academia-Government

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ To strengthen collaboration and work as one, it is expected that Q-STAR, an industry-led organization, and QIH, an organization centered on public research institutions and universities, will share information, interact, and collaborate more extensively than before.
- ✓ To this end, we will promote initiatives to strengthen the industry-academia-government collaboration framework. This includes establishing regular forums for systematic information sharing and exchange between Q-STAR and QIH, as well as building a new partnership framework (tentantivealymedQ-Partnership)that facilitates personnel exchanges and other collaborative activities.
- ✓ Progress and outcomes from national projects will also be actively shared in these forums to enhance and strengthen future industry-academia-government collaborative efforts for technology development and commercialization.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text: ### 〇Standardization, Intellectual Property Protection, Benchmark Setting, etc.

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Given the anticipated massive future market for the quantum industry, strategically pursuing standardization and intellectual property protection is crucial for Japan to gain overseas markets and leverage quantum technology as a driver of economic growth, thereby enhancing international competitiveness.
- ✓ Therefore, industry, academia, and government will work together to identify technologies requiring standardization for quantum devices (including materials and components) such as quantum computers, quantum sensors, and quantum communication equipment. We will then collaborate with existing standardization frameworks and stakeholders to vigorously and strategically advance standardization efforts, strongly influencing the formation of market rules.
- ✓ Quantum technology innovation hubs will also strengthen their efforts, such as actively and strategically pursuing intellectual property rights for quantum technologies, similar to other cutting-edge technologies, while referring to policies such as the "Intellectual Property Promotion Plan" 10 (<>).
- ✓ Furthermore, we will actively examine, establish, and provide benchmark indicators (performance, cost, convenience, low-carbonization, etc.) regarding the effects and performance of quantum devices from both business and technical perspectives (including the advantages of quantum technology over existing technologies), which are important for users.

text: ### 〇 Initiatives for Building a Strategic Supply Chain

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ To steadily advance the R&D and commercialization of quantum computers, quantum measurement/sensing/quantum materials, and quantum networks, it is crucial to build a strategic supply chain that stably supplies advanced devices, components, materials, etc.
✓ Therefore, industry, academia, and government will collaborate to identify and secure critical devices and components that Japan must ensure.
and components/materials that Japan should secure, develop a supply chain map, and build stable supply chains for these items from a strategic perspective.
- ✓ In doing so, we will also examine the development of devices unique or common to quantum computers, quantum measurement/sensing, and quantum networks, as well as the utilization of off-the-shelf products, considering factors such as reducing business risks, building stable supply chains, and economic rationality.
- ✓ Furthermore, we will build a broad-based industrial ecosystem by identifying key players (including SMEs) and form a broad-based market with high economic ripple effects.

text: ### 〇Platform Strategy and Collaborative Environment Development

doc_id: JPN_2023_Strategy of Quantum Future Industry Development
text:
- ✓ Quantum technologies, such as quantum computers, have yet to settle on definitive technical approaches or winning strategies. Therefore, a platform strategy—aimed at securing foundational technologies (platform technologies) that will be universally necessary regardless of future technical directions—is also crucial. To this end, industry, academia, and government will collaborate toexaminetheplatformtechnologiesJapanshouldprioritizeasanindustry,in parallelwithdiscussionsonthe aforementioned supplychainmap.Basedonthisexamination,wewill developaplatformstrategythatincludesconsiderationsforestablishingthedevelopmentandmanufacturing foundationsforthesetechnologies.
- ✓ Furthermore, the practical application and scaling of future quantum computers willrequiremassive investment.Giventheuncertaintysurroundingfuturetechnicalapproaches,relyingsolelyonindividual privatecompaniestotacklethiscanbehigh-riskinsomecases.Therefore, it is also important to actively provide industrial support, considering both areasforcollaborationand areas for competition.For instance, areas where collaboration among multiple companies is effective and efficient include sharing processes for prototyping, testing, and evaluation of semiconductors and other components; developing common technologies; building supply chains for common parts; setting benchmarks; and standardization.
- ✓ Therefore, under industry-academia-government collaboration, while considering initiatives to be implemented in both cooperative and competitive domains, we will establish frameworks and mechanisms enabling multiple companies to collaborate through open innovation in cooperative domains, along with providing necessary support.
